question: How do you declare a relation endpoint in `charmcraft.yaml` and what fields does it support?

claude_output.result:
In `charmcraft.yaml`, you declare relation endpoints under one of three top-level keys depending on the **role**:

| Key | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`provides`** | Endpoints your charm offers to other charms |
| **`requires`** | Endpoints your charm needs from other charms |
| **`peers`** | Endpoints for peer-to-peer relations between units of the same application |

## Structure

All three use the same schema:

```yaml
<role>:
  <endpoint-name>:
    interface: <interface-name>
    limit: <n>
    optional: true | false
    scope: global | container
```

## Supported fields

### `interface` *(required, string)*
The interface schema the relation conforms to. Usually defined by the author of the charm providing the interface.

**Constraints:**
- Cannot be `juju` or begin with `juju-`
- Must only contain characters `a-z` and `-`
- Cannot start with `-`

### `limit` *(optional, integer)*
The maximum number of supported connections to this relation endpoint.

### `optional` *(optional, boolean — default: `false`)*
Whether the relation is required. Not enforced by Juju itself, but used by other tools (e.g. `charmcraft`) and should always be included.

### `scope` *(optional, string — default: `global`)*
The set of units reported to the unit as members of the integration. Possible values:

- **`global`** — All remote units from integrated applications are visible.
- **`container`** — Only a single principal ↔ subordinate unit pair is visible. Used for subordinate charms.

## Full example

```yaml
provides:
  self:
    interface: identity

requires:
  parent:
    interface: birth
    limit: 2
    optional: false
    scope: global

peers:
  friend:
    interface: life
    limit: 150
    optional: true
    scope: container
```

> **Reference:** [Charmcraft YAML file – relation endpoints](https://documentation.ubuntu.com/charmcraft/en/latest/reference/files/charmcraft-yaml-file/)
